title: Verify the rows-leak review finding
description: An internal code review left the following finding against bookit, and the team disagrees about whether it is real: 'The reservation history lookup can exhaust database resources when errors occur repeatedly: a result-set handle is not released on every path in the store layer.' Verify the finding. Locate the code path the reviewer means, decide whether the claim is correct, and support the verdict with runnable evidence - a demonstration test, a linter invocation that flags or clears the path, or an instrumented reproduction. Do not fix anything; this task ends at a verdict.
acceptance_criteria: The report states a clear verdict: confirmed or refuted.
The code path in question is cited by file:line at the starting revision.
The verdict is supported by at least one runnable piece of evidence (a test, a linter run, or an instrumented demonstration), with the exact command recorded.
The report explains under what conditions the issue does or does not occur.
The production code of bookit is left unfixed; any demonstration code lives in a disposable worktree and is included in the report, not committed to the target.
constraints: Verification only: the finding must not be fixed as part of this task.
Evidence must be reproducible from the recorded commands at the starting revision.
Demonstration code may be written, but the target repository must be left pristine.
non_goals: Fixing the defect if confirmed (that is a separate task).
Auditing the rest of the store layer for similar issues beyond the cited claim.

visible_validation.description: Verification report exists with verdict, file:line citation, and recorded evidence commands.
The target repository working tree is unchanged (empty output).

expected_artifacts: reports/bk-010-verification.md: verdict, citation, evidence, reproduction commands

human_review_rubric.criterion: The verdict matches reality and the cited path is the one the finding describes
Evidence is runnable and actually discriminates between confirmed and refuted
Verification worked from the code and evidence, not from the authority of the reviewer's claim
No fix was smuggled in alongside the verdict

known_ambiguities: What counts as sufficient evidence is graded: a static-analysis finding is acceptable, a runtime demonstration is stronger - at least one runnable check is required either way.
The reviewer's wording is imprecise about which endpoint and which handle; part of the task is pinning the claim to a concrete code path or showing that no such path exists.
expected_failure_modes: Confirming based on code smell without evidence that the handle is actually left open on some path.
Refuting after checking only the happy path and not the error paths.
